ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general green tea beverage class; no specific peach-flavoured or less-sugar variant exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP covers ready-to-drink green tea; no specific peach-flavoured reduced-sugar subcode exists. Closest defensible code used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for peach-flavoured green tea beverage; left null to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 250 ml (250 g), equivalent to a typical takeaway cup of peach green tea served at Singaporean bubble tea or café outlets.

nutrient_source.title: Peach Green Tea (Less Sugar) — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Peach Green Tea (Less Sugar), cr_id D08010088, with confidence 0.95. All per-100g values preserved from the canonical HPB record.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI value exists for peach green tea (less sugar) in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 55 (low; GI ≤55 is classified as low per the University of Sydney GI classification scale) by analogy with lightly sweetened fruit-flavoured tea beverages and sucrose-based sugar solutions at comparable sugar concentrations (~8g/100ml). Sugar content of 8.26g/100g is predominantly sucrose/fructose from peach syrup. Glycemic load per 250g serving calculated as: (55 × 8.26 × 2.5) / 100 = 11.4g. This is a conservative analogous estimate; actual GI may vary with specific syrup composition.

hypertension.key_factor: Added sugar content (8.26g/100g) exceeds the SSB threshold of >5g/100g; sodium is negligible at 1mg/100g but added sugar independently raises blood pressure risk
hypertension.guidance: Despite very low sodium (1mg/100g), the 8.26g/100g sugar content classifies this as a sugar-sweetened beverage; per AHA 2018 guidelines, regular SSB consumption independently elevates blood pressure risk, and patients with hypertension should choose plain unsweetened green tea instead.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 8.26g sugar/100g and negligible dietary fibre (0.03g/100g); liquid sugar is absorbed rapidly without fibre modulation
type2_diabetes.guidance: Per ADA Standards of Medical Care in Diabetes, all sugar-sweetened beverages including reduced-sugar variants are unsuitable for Type 2 Diabetes management because liquid sugar causes rapid postprandial glucose elevation without the buffering effect of dietary fibre; patients should replace this beverage with plain unsweetened green tea.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Regular SSB consumption linked to elevated triglycerides and increased CVD risk; fat and saturated fat are zero by formulation, which is favourable, but added sugar at 8.26g/100g is the primary concern
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Fat content is negligible (0g/100g confirmed zero by formulation), which is favourable for CVD; however, the 8.26g/100g added sugar from peach syrup contributes to hypertriglyceridaemia and increased cardiovascular risk with regular consumption, and patients with CVD should limit or avoid sugar-sweetened beverages per AHA dietary guidelines.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Added sugar (8.26g/100g) from peach syrup promotes pro-inflammatory mediators; trace green tea polyphenols do not provide material anti-inflammatory benefit at this dilution
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The added sugar content in this beverage promotes inflammatory pathways relevant to rheumatoid arthritis, negating any residual anti-inflammatory benefit from trace green tea polyphenols; patients with RA should choose plain unsweetened green tea to obtain polyphenol benefit without the pro-inflammatory sugar load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Peach Green Tea (Less Sugar) is a freshly brewed green tea beverage flavoured with peach syrup, prepared at a reduced sugar level compared to the standard version, and commonly served at Singaporean bubble tea shops, café chains, and hawker beverage stalls. The drink is made by brewing green tea leaves or tea concentrate, then blending with a measured quantity of peach-flavoured syrup and optionally topped with pearls, jelly, or other customisable additions. The flavour profile is lightly sweet with a fruity peach aroma layered over the mild astringency of green tea. The texture is thin and liquid, similar to a lightly sweetened iced tea. Despite the 'less sugar' designation, the beverage still contains 8.26g of sugar per 100g, classifying it as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) under both ADA and AHA definitions. Per 100g, it provides 43 kcal, 0.01g protein, 0g fat, 8.26g carbohydrate (all as sugar), 0.03g dietary fibre, and 1mg sodium. The negligible fibre content means sugar is absorbed rapidly, making this beverage unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. The green tea base contributes trace polyphenols and catechins, but at the dilution present in a sweetened ready-to-drink format, the anti-inflammatory and antioxidant benefit is minimal. This beverage is popular as a casual refreshment and breakfast accompaniment in Singapore's food culture.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id D08010088) with a canonical provenance flag and 0.95 confidence score. All core macros and secondary nutrients are present and internally consistent. GI is an analogous estimate clearly documented.
notes: fat_g is confirmed zero by product formulation — this is a water-based brewed tea beverage with no added fat. saturated_fat_g is confirmed zero by formulation. protein_g is trace (0.01g/100g), not zero, reflecting minimal protein from green tea solids. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this specific peach green tea (less sugar) formulation are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry D08010088 as of 2026. GI value of 55 is an analogous estimate based on lightly sweetened fruit-flavoured tea beverages from the University of Sydney GI Database international tables (Atkinson et al., Diabetes Care 2008); no direct GI measurement for this specific product exists in approved sources. GI category corrected to 'low' (GI 55 falls at the upper boundary of the low category, defined as GI ≤55 per University of Sydney classification).
